generated_oml:
// C1: Physical Sensing Components
instance MotorEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Motor encoder mounted directly on the permanent magnet synchronous motor to measure motor angle (rotational position) of the first vertical axis in the MABI MAX 100 robot."
]

instance SecondaryEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Secondary encoder mounted behind the gearbox to measure the gearwheel's output angle (rotational position) of the first vertical axis in the MABI MAX 100 robot."
]

// C2: Produced Data from Sensing Components
instance MotorAngleData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder
]

instance GearwheelAngleData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecondaryEncoder
]

instance InputCurrentData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder
]

// C3: Measured Data Traces
instance MeasuredMotorAngle : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Measured motor output angle (r_M) from the motor encoder, representing the rotational position of the permanent magnet synchronous motor in the first axis of the MABI MAX 100 robot."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorAngleData
]

instance MeasuredGearwheelAngle : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Measured gearwheel output angle (r_G) from the secondary encoder, representing the rotational position after the gear transmission in the first axis of the MABI MAX 100 robot."
    DTDFVocab:fromData GearwheelAngleData
]

instance MeasuredInputCurrent : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Measured input current (I_M) from the motor encoder, representing the electrical current flowing into the permanent magnet synchronous motor in the first axis of the MABI MAX 100 robot."
    DTDFVocab:fromData InputCurrentData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance MQTTStreamingProtocol : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder, SecondaryEncoder
    DTDFVocab:producedData MeasuredMotorAngle, MeasuredGearwheelAngle, MeasuredInputCurrent
]

// C5: Engineering Models
instance StructuralSysMLModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Block Definition Diagram (BDD) and Internal Block Diagram (IBD) describing the first vertical axis of the MABI MAX 100 industrial robot as a composition of Motor, Gearwheel, and Cycloidal Gearbox components with rotational (torque, angular velocity) and current flow definitions."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingTool
]

instance MotorSimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink model simulating the motor behavior, including frictionless and friction-affected conditions (Coulomb-Viscous, Viscous, Stribeck, Dahl, LuGre)."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABSimulink
]

instance GearwheelSimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink model simulating the gearwheel behavior, incorporating gear transmission dynamics and friction effects."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABSimulink
]

instance CycloidalGearboxSimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink model simulating the cycloidal gearbox behavior, accounting for its complex mechanical structure and friction characteristics."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABSimulink
]

// C6: Simulated Data Traces
instance SimulatedMotorAngleData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorSimulationModel
]

instance SimulatedGearwheelAngleData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GearwheelSimulationModel
]

instance SimulatedInputCurrentData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorSimulationModel
]

instance SimulatedMotorAngle : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated motor output angle (δr_M) derived from Coulomb-Viscous friction models in MATLAB Simulink for comparison with measured data."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SimulatedMotorAngleData
]

instance SimulatedGearwheelAngle : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated gearwheel output angle (δr_G) derived from gear transmission simulation models in MATLAB Simulink for comparison with measured data."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SimulatedGearwheelAngleData
]

instance SimulatedInputCurrent : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated input current (δI_M) derived from motor dynamics simulation models in MATLAB Simulink for comparison with measured data."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SimulatedInputCurrentData
]

instance SimulatedTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated torque values derived from dynamics models in MATLAB Simulink for friction analysis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationProcessingPipeline
]

// C7: Data Processing Pipeline
instance SimulationProcessingPipeline : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Processing pipeline that compares measured vs. simulated data traces (e.g., r_M, r_G) using Coulomb-Viscous friction models to identify friction-induced inaccuracies (up to 30%)."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionAnalysisService
]

// C8: Services
instance FrictionAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time friction identification service that continuously analyzes joint friction using white-box (analytical) and black-box (data-driven) models (Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck, Dahl, LuGre) to refine friction models dynamically."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeFrictionInsight
]

instance AdaptiveControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Model-based control optimization service that enables adaptive control algorithms to adjust robot motion parameters (torque, velocity) for improved accuracy based on friction analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides MotionParameterAdjustment
]

instance DashboardVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Visualization dashboard service that aggregates and displays data traces (I_M, r_M, r_G, r_out) for real-time comparison of measured vs. simulated behavior, facilitating quick identification of friction trends and model parameter updates."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataTraceVisualization
]

// C9: Insights and Actions
instance RealTimeFrictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time comparison of measured vs. simulated data traces (e.g., r_M, r_G) identifies friction-induced inaccuracies (up to 30%) in the robot's first axis, enabling immediate detection of friction trends."
]

instance FrictionModelUpdate : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Continuous model updates to dynamically adapt friction models during operation to account for wear, tooling changes, or load variations, enabling real-time control adjustments."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance MotionParameterAdjustment : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Adaptive control algorithm adjustments to robot motion parameters (torque, velocity) based on friction analysis to improve motion accuracy."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C10: Enablers
instance SysMLModelingTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML (Block Definition Diagram and Internal Block Diagram) tool used for structural modeling of the first axis components (Motor, Gearwheel, Cycloidal Gearbox)."
    DTDFVocab:enables StructuralSysMLModel
]

instance MATLABSimulink : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink platform used for behavioral modeling and simulation of motor, gearwheel, and gearbox components with friction effects (Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck, Dahl, LuGre)."
    DTDFVocab:enables MotorSimulationModel, GearwheelSimulationModel, CycloidalGearboxSimulationModel
]

instance MQTTProtocol : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol enabling real-time data streaming from the robot's physical system to the digital twin via sensor streams (input current I_M, output angle)."
    DTDFVocab:enables MQTTStreamingProtocol
]

instance DashboardVisualization : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Unified dashboard visualization tool aggregating and presenting data traces (I_M, r_M, r_G, r_out) for real-time analysis and decision support."
    DTDFVocab:enables DashboardVisualizationService
]

instance DynamicsModel : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Robot-specific dynamics model derived from manufacturer data to calculate torque, angular velocity, and acceleration from measured inputs (position, velocity, acceleration)."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationProcessingPipeline
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The twinning time scale is **real-time and near-real-time**:
   - **Data acquisition frequency**: Sensor data (e.g., **I_M**, encoder readings) is streamed in **real-time** via MQTT, with no explicit sampling rate mentioned but implied to be **high-frequency** (e.g., kHz) for dynamic friction modeling.
   - **Model updates**: Friction models are dynamically refined during operation, with **continuous identification** of friction parameters (e.g., Coulomb friction coefficient, viscous damping) based on live data.
   - **Synchronization**: The DT processes data traces in **sub-second intervals** to maintain alignment with physical system dynamics."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT constellation is **focused on the first axis of the MABI MAX 100 robot**, with a **purpose-driven architecture**:
   - **Centralized processing**: The DT processes data traces (e.g., **I_M**, **r_M**, **r_G**, **r_out**) from the physical system in a **single digital shadow** (FrictionShadow) to derive friction-related insights.
   - **Hierarchical model integration**: The DT incorporates **white-box (analytical) and black-box (data-driven) friction models** (e.g., Simulink simulations) to enable **dynamic model refinement** during operation.
   - **Traceability-driven orchestration**: Data provenance is maintained via **metadata associations** (e.g., source origin, computation models) to ensure transparency in model validation."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The life cycle stages covered by the digital shadow include:
   - **As-manufactured phase**: The digital shadow is designed to capture the **physical structure and behavior** of the robot’s first axis (motor, gearbox) during **initial operation**, focusing on friction modeling and control accuracy.
   - **As-operated phase**: The DT continuously evolves during **robot operation** to account for **wear, tooling changes, or load variations**, enabling **dynamic friction adaptation** and **motion accuracy improvements**."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The fidelity and validity considerations include:
   - **Model validation**: Friction models (white-box/black-box) are validated against **real-time sensor data** (e.g., **I_M**, encoder readings) to ensure accuracy in predicting friction-induced inaccuracies.
   - **Uncertainty quantification**: The difference between **measured vs. simulated** data traces (e.g., **r_M**, **r_G**) is used to quantify friction-related errors, with **traceability** ensuring model reliability.
   - **Verification/validation mechanisms**:
     - **SysML models** (BDD, IBD) verify structural consistency.
     - **Simulink simulations** validate behavioral models (e.g., friction laws).
     - **Dashboard comparisons** validate real-time friction identification against physical observations."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT technical connection includes:
   - **MQTT protocol**: Enables **real-time bidirectional data exchange** between the physical robot and the DT.
   - **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)**: Implied for future integration to standardize data exchange and interoperability with the physical system.
   - **Network architecture**: The DT processes data traces in a **centralized manner**, with no explicit decentralization mentioned."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The standardization includes:
   - **SysML**: Used for **structural and behavioral modeling** of the robot’s first axis.
   - **Digital Shadow Reference Model (DSRM)**: Follows a **UML-based reference model** (Fig. 1) for defining data traces, sources, assets, and processing components.
   - **MQTT protocol**: Standardized for **real-time data streaming** between the physical system and DT.
   - **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)**: Implied for future **standardized interoperability** with the physical system."
]
